parsed_review.summary: CP00-499 is a clean, internally consistent descriptor-only governance closeout pack (RP16.P05.M06.S21-RP16.P06.M03.S10, 150 units, risk C). Independently verified: (1) deliverable distribution counted directly from the manifest matches the registry requirements, contract projection, and prompt baseline exactly (implementation 61, fixture 27, security_audit 23, test 17, claude_review 7, hermes_evidence 3, ui 12 = 150); (2) implementation in packages/governance/src is pure frozen-descriptor construction with no fs/network/exec/process.env usage, confirming the no-write/no-real-data boundary at the code level in addition to the GOVERNANCE_CORE_CP499_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION; (3) the governance contract current_pack and p05_closeout_p06_foundation_descriptor projection match the registry binding and descriptor (section_count 9, all rows descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false); (4) dedicated CP499 tests assert coverage, descriptor-only rows, and authority boundaries, and command-evidence records all gate validators plus npm test/validate and git diff --check at exit 0. Authority boundary is enforced in code, tests, and contract: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, human_final_approval_required=true. No over-claim of production readiness (manifest/command-evidence: production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status implemented_pending_review; production_ready_candidate is correctly gated on coverage+slice validity). Hermes patterns are used only for the deterministic evidence-packet/receipt descriptor (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. Review hardening: auth preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON), no prior raw-output/receipt artifacts and no preserved invalid attempts, so no malformed/fenced/tool-shaped/0-byte review evidence exists to reject. Unrelated dirty UI/workbook files and the LDIP invalid-attempt artifact are correctly scoped out. Claude remains a read-only reviewer and not the final approver.
